title: lindernia dubia var. inundata
intro: Lindernia dubia var. inundata, also known as water-purslane or swampseed, is a plant species belonging to the Linderniaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and skin infections. The health benefits of Lindernia dubia var. inundata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a decoction, made by boiling the roots and leaves in water, or used as a topical application to aid in skin and wound healing.
